hypothesis: Classifying TRIP as Consumer Discretionary instead of Unknown may improve allocation quality by routing an existing production-universe ticker through the same sector-aware sizing and event-distance rules as peer travel/consumer names, instead of treating it as plain risk-on inventory.
why_not_llm_soft_ranking: LLM soft-ranking attribution remains sample-limited, so this tested a deterministic production-shared allocation path that does not depend on LLM/news coverage.

parameters.baseline: TRIP absent from SECTOR_MAP, sector defaults to Unknown

market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull where strategy makes money but lags indexes
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape with lower win rate

gate4_basis: Rejected: no fixed-window metric moved, so the metadata change did not improve the tradable allocation path.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: This was an alternate deterministic alpha search because LLM ranking data is still thin.

history_guardrails.why_not_simple_repeat: This did not retest raw universe expansion or broad sector sorting; it tested one existing production-universe ticker's sector-aware allocation path.

rejection_reason: TRIP sector classification did not change any accepted 3-window metrics, so it failed Gate 4 and was rolled back.
do_not_repeat_without_new_evidence: Do not re-add TRIP to SECTOR_MAP as an alpha change unless a new Consumer Discretionary rule would actually use it.
Do not infer that Unknown-sector metadata cleanup is a profitable allocation alpha from the current fixed-window evidence.
next_retry_requires: A broader point-in-time audit of Unknown-sector candidates showing repeated sizing/routing distortion.
A production-shared rule whose behavior demonstrably depends on the corrected sector metadata.
